pass  # Memory is best-effort; don't crash the session\n127\t\n128\t    def _apply_decay(self) -> bool:\n129\t        \"\"\"\n130\t        Apply time-based confidence decay to all facts.\n131\t        Facts unreferenced for 30+ days lose confidence: auto at 0.1/period, explicit at 0.05/period.\n132\t        Prunes facts whose confidence drops below DECAY_THRESHOLD.\n133\t        Returns True if any \"Compare these two facts about a user. similarity between two strings.\"\"\"\n398\t        if not s1 or not s2:\n399\t            return 0.0\n400\t        words1 = set(s1.split())\n401\t        words2 = set(s2.split())\n402\t        intersection = len(words1 & words2)\n403\t        union = len(words1 | words2)\n404\t        return intersection / union if union > 0 else 0.0\n405\t\n406\t    def _facts_hash(self) -> str:\n407\t )\n528\t                resp.raise_for_status()\n529\t                data = resp.json()\n530\t            embeddings = [item[\"embedding\"] for item in data[\"data\"]]\n531\t            topic_emb = facts\nEXPLICIT_DECAY_RATE = 0.05  # Slower decay for explicitly saved facts\n\n\nclass FactCategory(str, Enum):\n    \"\"\"Categories for user memory facts.\"\"\"\n    PREFERENCE = \"preference\"\n    PERSONAL = \"personal\"\n    CORRECTION = \"correction\"\n    CONTEXT = \"context\"\n\n\nclass FactSource(str, Enum):\n    \"\"\"Source of a memory fact.\"\"\"\n    AUTO = \"auto\"       # Automatically extracted from conversation\n    EXPLICIT = \"explicit\"  # Explicitly saved via save_memory tool\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass StructuredFact:\n    \"\"\"A structured memory fact with metadata.\"\"\"\n    text: str\n    category: FactCategory\n    confidence: float\n    source: FactSource\n    created_at: str\n    last_referenced: str\n\n    def to_dict(self) -> dict:\n \"created_at\": self.created_at,\n            \"last_referenced\": self.last_referenced,\n        }\n\n    @classmethod\n    def from_dict(cls, data: dict) -> \"StructuredFact\":\n        return    created_at=data[\"created_at\"],\n            last_referenced=data[\"last_referenced\"],\n        )\n\n\nclass UserMemoryStore:\n    def __init__(self, user_id: str, memory_dir: str = load(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Load memory from disk. No-op if file doesn't exist.\"\"\"\n        if self.file_path.exists():\n            try:\n                with open(self.file_path, \"r\") as f:\n                    self._data = json.load(f)\n            except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n                self._data = {}\n        if \"facts\" not in self._data:\n            self._data[\"facts\"] = []\n        if \"user_id\" not in self._data:\n            self._data[\"user_id\"] = self.user_id\n        if \"created_at\" not in self._data:\n            self._data[\"created_at\"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n\n f:\n                    # New structured format\n                    self._facts.append(StructuredFact.from_dict(f))\n                else:\n                    # Legacy format - migrate to new format\n                    self._facts.append(StructuredFact(\n                        text=f[\"text\"],\n                        category=FactCategory.CONTEXT,\n                        confidence=0.7,\n                        source=FactSource.AUTO,\n                        created_at=f.get(\"created_at\", datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()),\n                        last_referenced=f.get(\"created_at\", datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()),\n                    ))\n\n        # Apply decay and prune stale facts; persist if anything changed\n        if _apply_decay(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Apply time-based confidence decay to all facts.\n        Facts unreferenced for 30+ days lose confidence: auto at 0.1/period, explicit at 0.05/period.\n        Prunes facts whose confidence drops below DECAY_THRESHOLD.\n        Returns True if any facts were pruned.\n        \"\"\"\n        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n        surviving = []\n        for fact in self._facts:\n            try:\n                last_ref = datetime.fromisoformat(fact.last_referenced)\n                if last_ref.tzinfo is None:\n                    last_ref = last_ref.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)\n                days_stale = (now - last_ref).days\n            except (ValueError, TypeError):\n                days_stale = 0\n\n            if days_stale >= DECAY_PERIOD_DAYS:\n                periods = days_stale / DECAY_PERIOD_DAYS\n                rate = EXPLICIT_DECAY_RATE if fact.source == FactSource.EXPLICIT else AUTO_DECAY_RATE\n                fact.confidence = max(0.0, fact.confidence - rate * periods)\n\n            if fact.confidence >= DECAY_THRESHOLD:\n                surviving.append(fact)\n\n        pruned = len(surviving) < len(self._facts)\n        self._facts = surviving\n        return pruned\n\n    def _normalize_text(self, text: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Normalize text for comparison.\"\"\"\n        return \" \".join(text.lower().split())\n\n    def _is_duplicate(self, new_fact: str, category: FactCategory) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if a fact already exists (exact or near-exact match).\"\"\"\n        new_normalized = self._normalize_text(new_fact)\n        for fact in self._facts:\n            if fact.category == category:\n                existing_normalized = self._normalize_text(fact.text)\n                # Check for exact match or one containing the other\n                if new_normalized == existing_normalized:\n                    return True\n                # Near-exact: one is a prefix of the other with significant overlap\n                if len(new_normalized) > 20 and len(existing_normalized) > 20:\n                    shorter, longer = sorted([new_normalized, existing_normalized], key=len)\n                    if longer.startswith(shorter) or shorter in longer:\n                        return True\n        return False\n\n    def add_fact(\n        self,\n        text: str,\n        category: FactCategory = FactCategory.CONTEXT,\n        confidence: float = 0.7,\n        source: FactSource = FactSource.AUTO,\n    ) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Add a structured fact about the user with deduplication and contradiction detection.\n        Returns True if fact was added, False if duplicate/skipped.\n        Enforces MAX_FACTS limit.\n        \"\"\"\n        text = text.strip()\n        if not text:\n            return False\n\n        # Explicit corrections always override with full confidence\n        if source == FactSource.EXPLICIT and category == FactCategory.CORRECTION:\n            confidence = 1.0\n\n        # Deduplication: skip if exact or near-exact match exists\n        if self._is_duplicate(text, category):\n            return False\n\n        # Contradiction detection: remove same-category facts that likely conflict.\n        # Explicit saves use a lower threshold (more aggressive replacement) since\n        # the user is intentionally correcting information.\n        # CORRECTION category also checks across all categories.\n        new_normalized = self._normalize_text(text)\n        sim_threshold = 0.2 if source == FactSource.EXPLICIT else 0.4\n        categories_to_check = list(FactCategory) if category == FactCategory.CORRECTION else [category]\n\n        facts_to_remove = []\n        for fact in self._facts:\n            if fact.category in categories_to_check:\n                existing_normalized = self._normalize_text(fact.text)\n                if self._similarity_ratio(new_normalized, existing_normalized) >= sim_threshold:\n                    facts_to_remove.append(fact)\n        for fact in facts_to_remove:\n            self._facts.remove(fact)\n\n        # Trim oldest facts if at limit\n        if len(self._facts) >= MAX_FACTS:\n            self._facts = self._facts[-(MAX_FACTS - 1):]\n\n        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n        fact = StructuredFact(\n            text=text,\n            category=category,\n            confidence=confidence,\n            source=source,\n            created_at=now,\n            last_referenced=now,\n        )\n        self._facts.append(fact)\n        self.save()\n        return True\n\n    def get_facts(self) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Return list of fact strings, updating last_referenced timestamps.\"\"\"\n        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n        for fact in self._facts:\n            fact.last_referenced = now\n        return [f.text for f in self._facts]\n\n    def self._facts:\n            fact.last_referenced = now\n        return self._facts.copy()\n\n    async def extract_and_save(\n        self,\n        user_text: str,\n        assistant_text: str,\n turn and save them.\n\n        Calls gpt-5.4-nano with a focused extraction prompt to identify user-specific\n        facts worth persisting (location, preferences, interests, personal details).\n        Returns structured facts with category and confidence.\n        Fire-and-forget safe — all errors are suppressed to avoid disrupting the session.\n        \"\"\"\n        if not details (e.g., 'lives in Berlin', 'has two cats')\\n\"\n            \"- correction: User correcting previous assistant behavior\\n\"\n            \"- context: Other contextual information (e.g., 'working on Python project')\\n\\n\"\n            \"Confidence scoring:\\n\"\n            \"- 0.9-1.0: Explicitly stated preference or fact\\n\"\n            \"- 0.7-0.9: Reasonably inferred from conversation\\n\\n\"\n            \"Return ONLY a valid JSON array of objects with 'text', 'category', and 'confidence', e.g.\\n\"\n            '[{\"text\": \"User lives in Paris\", \"category\": \"personal\", \"confidence\": 0.9}, '\n            '{\"text\": \"User prefers metric units\", \"category\": \"preference\", \"confidence\": 0.85}]\\n'\n            \"Return [] if nothing is worth saving.\"\n        )\n        turn = f\"[User]: {user_text}\\n[Assistant]: {assistant_text}\"\n\n        try:\n            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=30.0) as client:\n                resp = await client.post(\n                    \"https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions\",\n                    headers={\n                        \"Authorization\": f\"Bearer {api_key}\",\n                        \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n                    },\n                    json={\n                        \"model\": \"gpt-5.4-nano\",\n                        \"messages\": [\n                            {\"role\": \"system\", \"content\": prompt},\n                            {\"role\": \"user\", \"content\": turn},\n                        ],\n                        \"temperature\": 0,\n                        \"max_tokens\": 512,\n                    },\n                )\n                resp.raise_for_status()\n                data = resp.json()\n\n                # Track token usage if cost_tracker is provided\n                if cost_tracker is not None and session_id is not None:\n                    usage = data.get(\"usage\", {})\n                    if usage:\n                        try:\n                            await cost_tracker.track_usage(\n                                session_id=session_id,\n                                model=\"gpt-5.4-nano\",\n                                input_tokens=usage.get(\"prompt_tokens\", 0),\n                                output_tokens=usage.get(\"completion_tokens\", 0),\n                            )\n                        except Exception:\n                            pass  # Never crash the session over cost tracking\n                        if on_backend_cost is not None:\n                            try:\n                                await on_backend_cost(\n                                    \"gpt-5.4-nano\",\n                                    usage.get(\"prompt_tokens\", 0),\n                                    usage.get(\"completion_tokens\", 0),\n                                )\n                            except Exception:\n                                pass\n\n                content = data[\"choices\"][0][\"message\"][\"content\"].strip()\n                facts = json.loads(content)\n                if isinstance(facts, Exception:\n            pass  # Extraction is best-effort; never crash the session\n\n    async def semantic_compare(\n        self,\n        text1: str,\n        text2: str,\n        api_key: str,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Use gpt-5.4-nano to compare two facts semantically.\n        Returns: 'duplicate' | 'contradiction' | 'refinement' | 'distinct'\n        \"\"\"\n        prompt = (\n            \"Compare these two facts about a user. Return ONLY one word:\\n\"\n            \"- 'duplicate' if they express the same information\\n\"\n            \"- 'contradiction' if they directly contradict each other\\n\"\n            \"- 'refinement' if Fact 2 is a more specific or updated version of Fact 1\\n\"\n            \"- 'distinct' if they are unrelated or independently useful\\n\\n\"\n            f\"Fact 1: {text1}\\nFact 2: {text2}\"\n        )\n        try:\n            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=10.0) as client:\n                resp = await client.post(\n                    \"https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions\",\n                    headers={\n                        \"Authorization\": f\"Bearer {api_key}\",\n                        \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n                    },\n                    json={\n                        \"model\": \"gpt-5.4-nano\",\n                        \"messages\": [{\"role\": \"user\", \"refinement\", \"distinct\"):\n                    return result\n                return \"distinct\"\n        except Exception:\n            return \"distinct\"  # Fallback to safe default\n\n    def _similarity_ratio(self, s1: str, s2: str) -> float:\n        \"\"\"Calculate simple Jaccard similarity between two strings.\"\"\"\n        if not s1 or not s2:\n            return 0.0\n        words1 = set(s1.split())\n        words2 = set(s2.split())\n        intersection = len(words1 & words2)\n        union = len(words1 | words2)\n        return intersection / union if union > 0 else 0.0\n\n    def _facts_hash(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Compute a hash of current facts for summary cache invalidation.\"\"\"\n        content = json.dumps([f.to_dict() for f in self._facts], sort_keys=True)\n        return hashlib.md5(content.encode()).hexdigest()\n\n    async def get_or_generate_summary(\n        self,\n        api_key: str,\n        cost_tracker=None,\n        session_id: str | None = None,\n        on_backend_cost=None,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Return a cached one-paragraph user summary, regenerating if facts changed.\"\"\"\n        if not self._facts:\n            return \"\"\n        current_hash = self._facts_hash()\n        if (\n            self._data.get(\"summary_facts_hash\") == current_hash\n            and   if summary:\n            self._data[\"user_summary\"] = summary\n            self._data[\"summary_facts_hash\"] = current_hash\n            self.save()\n        return summary\n\n    async def _generate_summary(\n        self,\n        api_key: str,\n        cost_tracker=None,\n        session_id: str | None = None,\n        on_backend_cost=None,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Generate a Focus on the most important details. \"\n            \"Write in third person (e.g., 'The user...').     \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n                    },\n                    json={\n                        \"model\": \"gpt-5.4-nano\",\n                        \"messages\": [{\"role\": resp.raise_for_status()\n                data = resp.json()\n                if cost_tracker is not None and session_id is not None:\n                    usage = data.get(\"usage\", {})\n data[\"choices\"][0][\"message\"][\"content\"].strip()\n        except Exception:\n            return \"\"\n\n    def get_facts_by_topic(self, topic: str) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Return facts relevant to topic using keyword substring matching.\"\"\"\n        topic_words = [w.lower() for w in topic.split() if len(w) > 3]\n        if not topic_words:\n            return []\n        matched = []\n        for fact in self._facts:\n            fact_lower = fact.text.lower()\n            if any(word in fact_lower for word in topic_words):\n                matched.append(fact.text)\n        return matched\n\n    async def get_facts_by_topic_async(self, topic: str, api_key: str) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Return topic-relevant facts. Uses keyword matching, falls back to embeddings.\"\"\"\n        matches = self.get_facts_by_topic(topic)\n        if matches:\n            return matches\n        return await self._get_facts_by_embedding(topic, api_key)\n\n    async def _get_facts_by_embedding(self, topic: str, api_key: str) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Use OpenAI text-embedding-3-small for cosine similarity retrieval.\"\"\"\n        if not self._facts:\n            return []\n        fact_texts = [f.text for f in self._facts]\n        try:\n            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=30.0) as client:\n                resp = await client.post(\n                    \"https://api.openai.com/v1/embeddings\",\n                    headers={\n                        \"Authorization\": f\"Bearer {api_key}\",\n                        \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n                    },\n                    json={\n                        \"model\": \"text-embedding-3-small\",\n                        \"input\": [topic] + fact_texts,\n                    },\n                )\n                resp.raise_for_status()\n                data = resp.json()\n            embeddings = [item[\"embedding\"] for item in data[\"data\"]]\n            topic_emb = embeddings[0]\n            fact_embs = embeddings[1:]\n\n            def cosine_sim(a: list, b: list) -> float:\n                dot = sum(x * y for x, y in zip(a, b))\n                norm_a = sum(x * x for x in a) ** 0.5\n                norm_b = sum(x * x for x in b) ** 0.5\n                return dot / (norm_a * norm_b) if norm_a and norm_b else 0.0\n\n            THRESHOLD = 0.4\n            scored = sorted(\n                [(cosine_sim(topic_emb, emb), text) for emb, text in zip(fact_embs, fact_texts)],\n                reverse=True,\n            )\n            return [text for score, text in scored if score >= THRESHOLD]\n        except Exception:\n            return []\n\n    async def add_fact_with_semantic_dedup(\n        self,\n        text: str,\n        api_key: str,\n        category: FactCategory = FactCategory.CONTEXT,\n        confidence: float = 0.7,\n        source: FactSource = FactSource.AUTO,\n    ) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Add a fact with semantic deduplication using gpt-5.4-nano.\n        Falls back to simple dedup if API call fails.\n        Returns True if fact was added, False if duplicate/skipped.\n        \"\"\"\n        text = text.strip()\n        if not text:\n            return False\n\n        # First check simple dedup\n        if self._is_duplicate(text, category):\n            return False\n\n        # For borderline cases, use semantic comparison\n        new_normalized = self._normalize_text(text)\n        for fact in self._facts:\n            if fact.category == category:\n                existing_normalized = self._normalize_text(fact.text)\n                # If strings are somewhat similar but not exact, use semantic comparison\n                similarity = self._similarity_ratio(new_normalized, existing_normalized)\n                if 0.1 < similarity < 0.9:\n                    result = await self.semantic_compare(text, fact.text, api_key)\n                    if result == \"duplicate\":\n                        return False\n                    if result in (\"contradiction\", \"refinement\"):\n                        # Replace old fact with new (contradiction: new overrides; refinement: new is more specific)\n                        self._facts.remove(fact)\n                        break\n\n        # Trim oldest facts if at limit\n        if len(self._facts) >= MAX_FACTS:\n            self._facts = self._facts[-(MAX_FACTS - 1):]\n\n        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n        new_fact = StructuredFact(\n            text=text,\n            category=category,\n            confidence=confidence,\n            source=source,\n            created_at=now,\n            last_referenced=now,\n        )\n        self._facts.append(new_fact)\n        self.save()\n        return True\n","numLines":602,"startLine":1,"totalLines":602}}}

Session 1: Create memory store and save a fact\n107\t            store1 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_email, memory_dir=memory_dir)\n108\t            store1.load()\n109\t\n110\t            added = store1.add_fact(\n111\t                text=\"User prefers Celsius for temperature\",\n112\t                category=FactCategory.PREFERENCE,\n113\t                confidence=1.0,\n114\t                source=FactSource.EXPLICIT,\n115\t            )\n116\t            assert added, \"Fact should be added successfully\"\n117\t\n118\t            # Verify file was created\n119\t            import hashlib\n120\t            user_hash = hashlib.sha256(user_email.encode()).hexdigest()\n121\t            json_path = Path(memory_dir) / f\"{user_hash}.json\"\n122\t            assert json_path.exists(), \"Memory file should be created after save\"\n123\t\n124\t            # Verify the fact is in session 1\n125\t            facts1 = store1.get_facts()\n126\t            assert \"User prefers Celsius for temperature\" in facts1\n127\t\n128\t            # Session 2: Create a NEW store instance (simulating a new session/connection)\n129\t            store2 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_email, memory_dir=memory_dir)\n130\t            store2.load()\n131\t\n132\t            # Session 1: Add multiple facts\n150\t        store1 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_email, memory_dir=memory_dir)\n151\t        store1.load()\n152\t\n153\t        store1.add_fact(\"User lives in Berlin\", category=FactCategory.PERSONAL, confidence=0.9)\n154\t        store1.add_fact(\"User is a software engineer\", category=FactCategory.PERSONAL, confidence=0.95)\n155\t        store1.add_fact(\"User prefers dark mode\", category=FactCategory.PREFERENCE, confidence=1.0)\n156\t        store1.add_fact(\"User speaks German fluently\", category=FactCategory.PERSONAL, confidence=0.85)\n157\t\n158\t        facts1 = store1.get_facts()\n159\t        assert len(facts1) == 4\n160\t\n161\t        # Session 2: Load and build system message\n162\t        store2 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_email, memory_dir=memory_dir)\n163\t        store2.load()\n164\t\n165\t        # Build a system message like the real app does\n166\t        base_system = \"You are DUCK-E voice assistant.\"\n167\t        user_display = user_name or user_email\n168\t\n169\t        structured_facts = store2.get_structured_facts()\n170\t        memory_section = f\"\\n\\nThe current user is {user_display} ({user_email}).\"\n171\t\n172\t        if structured_facts:\n173\t            memory_section += \"\\nHere are things you remember about this user:\\n\"\n174\t            memory_section += \"\\n\".join(\n175\t                f\"- [{f.category.value}] {f.text}\" for f in structured_facts\n176\t            )\n177\t\n178\t        system_message = base_system + memory_section\n179\t\n180\t        # Verify all facts are in the system message\n181\t        assert \"User lives in Berlin\" in system_message\n182\t        assert \"User is a software engineer\" in system_message\n183\t        assert \"User prefers dark mode\" in system_message\n184\t        assert \"User speaks German fluently\" in system_message\n185\t\n186\t        # Verify categories are included\n187\t        assert \"[personal]\" in system_message\n188\t        assert \"[preference]\" in system_message\n189\t\n190\t    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n191\t    async def test_recall_memories_returns_saved_facts(\n192\t        self, memory_dir, test_user_headers\n193\t    ):\n194\t        \"\"\"\n195\t        E2E: The recall_memories tool returns facts saved in a prior session.\n196\t\n197\t        This simulates the tool call flow:\n198\t        1. Session 1: Save facts\n199\t        2. Session 2: Call recall_memories\n200\t        3. Verify facts are returned\n201\t        \"\"\"\n202\t        user_email = test_user_headers[\"x-forwarded-email\"]\n203\t\n204\t        # Session 1: Save facts with different categories\n205\t        store1 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_email, memory_dir=memory_dir)\n206\t        store1.load()\n207\t\n208\t        store1.add_fact(\"User loves sushi\", category=FactCategory.PREFERENCE, confidence=0.9, source=FactSource.EXPLICIT)\n209\t        store1.add_fact(\"User lives in Tokyo\", category=FactCategory.PERSONAL, confidence=0.95, source=FactSource.EXPLICIT)\n210\t        store1.add_fact(\"User is working on a Python project\", category=FactCategory.CONTEXT, confidence=0.8, source=FactSource.EXPLICIT)\n211\t\n212\t        # Session 2: Load and test recall_memories behavior\n213\t        store2 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_email, memory_dir=memory_dir)\n214\t        store2.load()\n215\t\n216\t        # Test: Get all facts (no topic filter)\n217\t        all_facts = store2.get_facts()\n218\t        assert len(all_facts) == 3\n219\t        assert \"User loves sushi\" in all_facts\n220\t        assert \"User lives in Tokyo\" in all_facts\n221\t        assert \"User is working on a Python project\" in all_facts\n222\t\n223\t        # Test: Get facts by topic (sushi-related)\n224\t        # Note: get_facts_by_topic uses keyword matching, word must be >3 chars\n225\t        sushi_facts = store2.get_facts_by_topic(\"sushi\")\n226\t        assert \"User loves sushi\" in sushi_facts\n227\t\n228\t        # Test: Get facts by topic (location-related)\n229\t        # \"Tokyo\" appears in the fact text\n230\t        tokyo_facts = store2.get_facts_by_topic(\"Tokyo\")\n231\t        assert \"User lives in Tokyo\" in tokyo_facts\n232\t\n233\t        # Test: Get structured facts with metadata\n234\t        structured_facts = store2.get_structured_facts()\n235\t        assert len(structured_facts) == 3\n236\t\n237\t        sushi_fact = next((f for f in structured_facts if \"sushi\" in f.text), None)\n238\t        assert sushi_fact is not None\n239\t        assert sushi_fact.category == FactCategory.PREFERENCE\n240\t        assert sushi_fact.confidence == 0.9\n241\t        assert sushi_fact.source == FactSource.EXPLICIT\n242\t\n243\t    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n244\t    async def test_memory_decay_reduces_confidence_over_time(\n245\t        self, memory_dir, test_user_headers\n246\t    ):\n247\t        \"\"\"\n248\t        E2E: Memory decay reduces confidence for facts not referenced recently.\n249\t\n250\t        This test verifies:\n251\t        1. Facts lose confidence over time based on DECAY_PERIOD_DAYS\n252\t        2. Auto-extracted facts decay faster (AUTO_DECAY_RATE) than explicit (EXPLICIT_DECAY_RATE)\n253\t        3. Facts below DECAY_THRESHOLD are pruned on load\n254\t        \"\"\"\n255\t        user_email = test_user_headers[\"x-forwarded-email\"]\n256\t\n257\t        # Session 1: Add facts with different sources\n258\t        store1 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_email, memory_dir=memory_dir)\n259\t        store1.load()\n260\t\n261\t        # Add an explicit fact (should decay slower)\n262\t        store1.add_fact(\n263\t            \"User prefers tea over coffee\",\n264\t            category=FactCategory.PREFERENCE,\n265\t            confidence=0.9,\n266\t            source=FactSource.EXPLICIT,\n267\t        )\n268\t\n269\t        # Add an auto-extracted fact (should decay faster)\n270\t        store1.add_fact(\n271\t            \"User is browsing a cooking website\",\n272\t            category=FactCategory.CONTEXT,\n273\t            confidence=0.7,\n274\t            source=FactSource.AUTO,\n275\t        )\n276\t\n277\t        # Manually age the facts by modifying last_referenced\n278\t        # Simulate 35 days ago (past one decay period of 30 days)\n279\t        old_date = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(days=35)).isoformat()\n280\t\n281\t        with open(store1.file_path, \"r\") as f:\n282\t            data = json.load(f)\n283\t\n284\t        for fact in data[\"facts\"]:\n285\t            fact[\"last_referenced\"] = old_date\n286\t\n287\t        with open(store1.file_path, \"w\") as f:\n288\t            json.dump(data, f, indent=2)\n289\t\n290\t        # Session 2: Load and verify decay was applied\n291\t        store2 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_email, memory_dir=memory_dir)\n292\t        store2.load()\n293\t\n294\t        structured_facts = store2.get_structured_facts()\n295\t\n296\t        # The auto-extracted fact should have decayed more\n297\t        # Initial: 0.7, after 35 days (1 period): 0.7 - 0.1 = 0.6\n298\t        # The explicit fact should have decayed less\n299\t        # Initial: 0.9, after 35 days (1 period): 0.9 - 0.05 = 0.85\n300\t        tea_fact = next((f for f in structured_facts if \"tea\" in f.text), None)\n301\t        cooking_fact = next((f for f in structured_facts if \"cooking\" in f.text), None)\n302\t\n303\t        # Both facts should still exist (above DECAY_THRESHOLD of 0.3)\n304\t        assert tea_fact is not None, \"Explicit fact should still exist after decay\"\n305\t        assert cooking_fact is not None, \"Auto fact should still exist after mild decay\"\n306\t\n307\t        # Explicit fact should have higher confidence than auto fact\n308\t        assert tea_fact.confidence > cooking_fact.confidence\n309\t\n310\t    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n311\t    async def test_memory_decay_prunes_old_facts(\n312\t        self, memory_dir, test_user_headers\n313\t    ):\n314\t        \"\"\"\n315\t        E2E: Facts with confidence below DECAY_THRESHOLD are pruned on load.\n316\t\n317\t        This test verifies that very old/stale facts are automatically removed\n318\t        when the memory store is loaded.\n319\t        \"\"\"\n320\t        user_email = test_user_headers[\"x-forwarded-email\"]\n321\t\n322\t        # Session 1: Add a low-confidence auto-extracted fact\n323\t        store1 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_email, memory_dir=memory_dir)\n324\t        store1.load()\n325\t\n326\t        store1.add_fact(\n327\t            \"User briefly mentioned something casual\",\n328\t            category=FactCategory.CONTEXT,\n329\t            confidence=0.35,  # Just above threshold\n330\t            source=FactSource.AUTO,\n331\t        )\n332\t\n333\t        # Age the fact significantly so it decays below threshold\n334\t        # 4 decay periods (120 days) with AUTO_DECAY_RATE of 0.1\n335\t        # 0.35 - (4 * 0.1) = -0.05 → below threshold\n336\t        old_date = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(days=120)).isoformat()\n337\t\n338\t        with open(store1.file_path, \"r\") as f:\n339\t            data = json.load(f)\n340\t\n341\t        for fact in data[\"facts\"]:\n342\t            fact[\"last_referenced\"] = old_date\n343\t\n344\t        with open(store1.file_path, \"w\") as f:\n345\t            json.dump(data, f, indent=2)\n346\t\n347\t        # Session 2: Load and verify fact was pruned\n348\t        store2 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_email, memory_dir=memory_dir)\n349\t        store2.load()\n350\t\n351\t        facts = store2.get_facts()\n352\t        assert \"User briefly mentioned something casual\" not in facts\n353\t\n354\t    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n355\t    async def test_memory_isolation_between_users(\n356\t        self, memory_dir\n357\t    ):\n358\t        \"\"\"\n359\t        E2E: Facts for one user do not bleed into another user's memory.\n360\t\n361\t        This verifies user isolation when using different email addresses.\n362\t        \"\"\"\n363\t        user_a_email = \"user-a@example.com\"\n364\t        user_b_email = \"user-b@example.com\"\n365\t\n366\t        # Verify isolation: User A should not see User B's facts\n377\t        store_a_reload = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_a_email, memory_dir=memory_dir)\n378\t        store_a_reload.load()\n379\t        facts_a = store_a_reload.get_facts()\n380\t        assert \"User A likes pizza\" in facts_a\n381\t        assert \"User B likes sushi\" not in facts_a\n382\t\n383\t        # Verify isolation: User B should not see User A's facts\n384\t        store_b_reload = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_b_email, memory_dir=memory_dir)\n385\t        store_b_reload.load()\n386\t        facts_b = store_b_reload.get_facts()\n387\t        assert \"User B likes sushi\" in facts_b\n388\t        assert \"User A likes pizza\" not in facts_b\n389\t\n390\t    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n391\t    async def test_contradiction_replacement_persists(\n392\t        self, memory_dir, test_user_headers\n393\t    ):\n394\t        \"\"\"\n395\t        E2E: When a fact is replaced due to contradiction, the new state persists.\n396\t\n397\t        This tests the contradiction detection and replacement flow.\n398\t        \"\"\"\n399\t        user_email = test_user_headers[\"x-forwarded-email\"]\n400\t\n401\t        # Session 1: Add more than MAX_FACTS (100)\n484\t        store = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_email, memory_dir=memory_dir)\n485\t        store.load()\n486\t\n487\t        # Add 105 facts with completely different text to avoid contradiction detection\n488\t        # The contradiction detection uses Jaccard similarity, and facts with similarity >= 0.4\n489\t        # would trigger removal of old facts. Using unrelated text prevents this.\n490\t        topics = [\n491\t            \"apple\", \"banana\", \"cherry\", \"date\", \"elderberry\", \"fig\", \"grape\", \"honeydew\",\n492\t            \"ice cream\", \"jelly\", \"kiwi\", \"lemon\", \"mango\", \"nectarine\", \"orange\", \"papaya\",\n493\t            \"quince\", \"raspberry\", \"strawberry\", \"tangerine\", \"ugli fruit\", \"vanilla\", \"watermelon\",\n494\t            \"xylophone\", \"yellow squash\", \"zucchini\", \"artichoke\", \"broccoli\", \"carrot\", \"daikon\",\n495\t            \"eggplant\", \"fennel\", \"garlic\", \"horseradish\", \"iceberg lettuce\", \"jalapeno\", \"kale\",\n496\t            \"leek\", \"mushroom\", \"nutmeg\", \"onion\", \"parsley\", \"quinoa\", \"radish\", \"spinach\",\n497\t            \"tomato\", \"ugli\", \"vanilla bean\", \"wasabi\", \"xanthan gum\", \"yam\", \"zest\"\n498\t        ]\n499\t\n500\t        # Generate 105 completely unique facts\n501\t        for i in range(105):\n502\t            topic = topics[i % len(topics)]\n503\t            # Make each fact unique by including a random element\n504\t            unique_text = f\"User mentioned {topic} during conversation {i} at time {i * 17}\"\n505\t            store.add_fact(unique_text, category=FactCategory.CONTEXT)\n506\t\n507\t        facts = store.get_facts()\n508\t        assert len(facts) == 100, f\"Should have exactly MAX_FACTS, got {len(facts)}\"\n509\t\n510\t        # The oldest facts should have been removed (they have low numbers in the text)\n511\t        fact_0_text = f\"User mentioned {topics[0]} during conversation 0 at time 0\"\n512\t        assert fact_0_text not in facts\n513\t\n514\t        # The newest fact should still be present\n515\t        fact_104_text = f\"User mentioned {topics[104 % len(topics)]} during conversation 104 at time 1768\"\n516\t        assert fact_104_text in facts\n517\t\n518\t    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n519\t    async def Session 2: Load and build system message\n        store2 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_email, memory_dir=memory_dir)\n        store2.load()\n\n        # Build a system message like the real app does\n        base_system = \"You are DUCK-E voice assistant.\"\n        user_display = user_name or user_email\n\n        structured_facts = store2.get_structured_facts()\n        memory_section = f\"\\n\\nThe current user is {user_display} ({user_email}).\"\n\n        if structured_facts:\n            memory_section += \"\\nHere are things you remember about this user:\\n\"\n            memory_section += \"\\n\".join(\n                f\"- [{f.category.value}] {f.text}\" for f in structured_facts\n            )\n\n        system_message = base_system + memory_section\n\n        # Session 1: Save facts\n        2. Session 2: Call recall_memories\n        3. Test: Get facts by topic (location-related)\n        # \"Tokyo\" appears in the fact text\n        tokyo_facts = store2.get_facts_by_topic(\"Tokyo\")\n        assert \"User lives in Tokyo\" in tokyo_facts\n\n        # Test: Get structured facts with metadata\n        structured_facts = store2.get_structured_facts()\n        assert len(structured_facts) == 3\n\n        sushi_fact = next((f for f in structured_facts if \"sushi\" in f.text), None)\n        assert sushi_fact is not None\n        assert sushi_fact.category == FactCategory.PREFERENCE\n        assert sushi_fact.confidence == 0.9\n        assert sushi_fact.source == FactSource.EXPLICIT\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_memory_decay_reduces_confidence_over_time(\n        self, memory_dir, test_user_headers\n    ):\n        \"\"\"\n        E2E: Memory decay reduces confidence for facts not referenced recently.\n\n        This test verifies:\n        1. Facts lose confidence over time based on DECAY_PERIOD_DAYS\n        2. Auto-extracted facts decay faster (AUTO_DECAY_RATE) than explicit (EXPLICIT_DECAY_RATE)\n        3. Facts below DECAY_THRESHOLD are pruned on load\n        \"\"\"\n        user_email = test_user_headers[\"x-forwarded-email\"]\n\n        # Session 1: Add facts with different sources\n        store1 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_email, memory_dir=memory_dir)\n        store1.load()\n\n        # Add an explicit fact (should decay slower)\n        store1.add_fact(\n            \"User prefers tea over coffee\",\n            category=FactCategory.PREFERENCE,\n            confidence=0.9,\n            source=FactSource.EXPLICIT,\n        )\n\n        # Add an auto-extracted fact (should decay faster)\n        store1.add_fact(\n            \"User is browsing a cooking website\",\n            category=FactCategory.CONTEXT,\n            confidence=0.7,\n            source=FactSource.AUTO,\n        )\n\n        # Manually age the facts by modifying last_referenced\n        # Simulate 35 days ago (past one decay period of 30 days)\n        old_date = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(days=35)).isoformat()\n\n        with open(store1.file_path, \"r\") as f:\n            data = json.load(f)\n\n        for fact in data[\"facts\"]:\n            fact[\"last_referenced\"] = old_date\n\n        with open(store1.file_path, \"w\") as f:\n            json.dump(data, f, indent=2)\n\n        # Session 2: Load and verify decay was applied\n        store2 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_email, memory_dir=memory_dir)\n        store2.load()\n\n        structured_facts = store2.get_structured_facts()\n\n        # The auto-extracted fact should have decayed more\n        # Initial: 0.7, after 35 days (1 period): 0.7 - 0.1 = 0.6\n        # The explicit fact should have decayed less\n        # Initial: 0.9, after 35 days (1 period): 0.9 - 0.05 = 0.85\n        tea_fact = next((f for f in structured_facts if \"tea\" in f.text), None)\n        cooking_fact = next((f for f in structured_facts if \"cooking\" in f.text), None)\n\n        # Both facts should still exist (above DECAY_THRESHOLD of 0.3)\n        assert tea_fact is not None, \"Explicit fact should still exist after decay\"\n        assert cooking_fact is not None, \"Auto fact should still exist after mild decay\"\n\n        # Explicit fact should have higher confidence than auto fact\n        assert tea_fact.confidence > cooking_fact.confidence\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_memory_decay_prunes_old_facts(\n        self, memory_dir, test_user_headers\n    ):\n        \"\"\"\n        E2E: Facts with confidence below DECAY_THRESHOLD are pruned on load.\n\n        This test verifies that very old/stale facts are automatically removed\n        when the memory store is loaded.\n        \"\"\"\n        user_email = test_user_headers[\"x-forwarded-email\"]\n\n        # Session 1: Add a low-confidence auto-extracted fact\n        store1 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_email, memory_dir=memory_dir)\n        store1.load()\n\n        store1.add_fact(\n            \"User briefly mentioned something casual\",\n            category=FactCategory.CONTEXT,\n            confidence=0.35,  # Just above threshold\n            source=FactSource.AUTO,\n        )\n\n        # Age the fact significantly so it decays below threshold\n        # 4 decay periods (120 days) with AUTO_DECAY_RATE of 0.1\n        # 0.35 - (4 * 0.1) = -0.05 → below threshold\n        old_date = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(days=120)).isoformat()\n\n        with open(store1.file_path, \"r\") as f:\n            data = json.load(f)\n\n        for fact in data[\"facts\"]:\n            fact[\"last_referenced\"] = old_date\n\n        with open(store1.file_path, \"w\") as f:\n            json.dump(data, f, indent=2)\n\n        # Session 2: Load and verify fact was pruned\n        store2 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_email, memory_dir=memory_dir)\n        store2.load()\n\n        facts = store2.get_facts()\n        assert \"User briefly mentioned something casual\" not in facts\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_memory_isolation_between_users(\n        self, memory_dir\n    ):\n        \"\"\"\n        E2E: Facts for one user do not bleed into another user's memory.\n\n        This verifies user isolation when using different email addresses.\n        \"\"\"\n        user_a_email = \"user-a@example.com\"\n        user_b_email = \"user-b@example.com\"\n\n        # User A saves facts\n        store_a = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_a_email, memory_dir=memory_dir)\n        store_a.load()\n        store_a.add_fact(\"User A likes pizza\", category=FactCategory.PREFERENCE)\n\n        # User B saves facts\n        store_b = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_b_email, memory_dir=memory_dir)\n        store_b.load()\n        store_b.add_fact(\"User B likes sushi\", category=FactCategory.PREFERENCE)\n\n        # Verify isolation: User A should not see User B's facts\n        store_a_reload = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_a_email, memory_dir=memory_dir)\n        store_a_reload.load()\n        facts_a = store_a_reload.get_facts()\n        assert \"User A likes pizza\" in facts_a\n        assert \"User B likes sushi\" not in facts_a\n\n        # Verify isolation: User B should not see User A's facts\n        store_b_reload = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_b_email, memory_dir=memory_dir)\n        store_b_reload.load()\n        facts_b = store_b_reload.get_facts()\n        assert \"User B likes sushi\" in facts_b\n        assert \"User A likes pizza\" not in facts_b\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_contradiction_replacement_persists(\n        self, memory_dir, test_user_headers\n    ):\n        \"\"\"\n        E2E: When a fact is replaced due to contradiction, the new state persists.\n\n        This tests the contradiction detection and replacement flow.\n        \"\"\"\n        user_email = test_user_headers[\"x-forwarded-email\"]\n\n        # Session 1: Add initial fact\n        store1 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_email, memory_dir=memory_dir)\n        store1.load()\n        store1.add_fact(\"User lives in NYC\", category=FactCategory.PERSONAL)\n\n        facts1 = store1.get_facts()\n        assert \"User lives in NYC\" in facts1\n\n        # Session 2: Add contradicting fact (simulating a correction)\n        store2 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_email, memory_dir=memory_dir)\n        store2.load()\n        store2.add_fact(\"User lives in Boston\", category=FactCategory.PERSONAL)\n\n        facts2 = store2.get_facts()\n        assert \"User lives in Boston\" in facts2\n        assert \"User lives in NYC\" not in facts2\n\n        # Summary is generated when facts exist\n        2. Summary is cached (facts_hash check)\n        3. Cached summary is used in new sessions\n        \"\"\"\n        user_email = test_user_headers[\"x-forwarded-email\"]\n        test_api_key = \"test-api-key\"\n\n        # Session 1: Add facts and generate summary\n        store1 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_email, memory_dir=memory_dir)\n        store1.load()\n\n        store1.add_fact(\"User lives in Berlin\", category=FactCategory.PERSONAL)\n        store1.add_fact(\"User is a software engineer\", category=FactCategory.PERSONAL)\n        store1.add_fact(\"User prefers dark mode\", category=FactCategory.PREFERENCE)\n\n        # Mock the OpenAI API for summary generation\n        async def mock_summary(*args, **kwargs):\n            return \"The user is a software engineer based in Berlin who prefers dark mode.\"\n\n        with patch.object(store1, \"_generate_summary\", mock_summary):\n            summary1 = await store1.get_or_generate_summary(test_api_key)\n\n        assert summary1\n        assert \"software engineer\" in summary1\n        assert \"Berlin\" in summary1\n\n        # Session 1: Add more than MAX_FACTS (100)\n        store = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_email, memory_dir=memory_dir)\n        store.load()\n\n        # Add 105 facts with completely different text to avoid contradiction detection\n        # The contradiction detection uses Jaccard similarity, and facts with similarity >= 0.4\n        # would trigger removal of old facts. Using unrelated text prevents this.\n        topics = [\n            \"apple\", \"banana\", \"cherry\", \"date\", \"elderberry\", \"fig\", \"grape\", \"honeydew\",\n            \"ice cream\", \"jelly\", \"kiwi\", \"lemon\", \"mango\", \"nectarine\", \"orange\", \"papaya\",\n            \"quince\", \"raspberry\", \"strawberry\", \"tangerine\", \"ugli fruit\", \"vanilla\", \"watermelon\",\n            \"xylophone\", \"yellow squash\", \"zucchini\", \"artichoke\", \"broccoli\", \"carrot\", \"daikon\",\n            \"eggplant\", \"fennel\", \"garlic\", \"horseradish\", \"iceberg lettuce\", \"jalapeno\", \"kale\",\n            \"leek\", \"mushroom\", \"nutmeg\", \"onion\", \"parsley\", \"quinoa\", \"radish\", \"spinach\",\n            \"tomato\", \"ugli\", \"vanilla bean\", \"wasabi\", \"xanthan gum\", \"yam\", \"zest\"\n        ]\n\n        # Generate 105 completely unique facts\n        for i in range(105):\n            topic = topics[i % len(topics)]\n            # Make each fact unique by including a random element\n            unique_text = f\"User mentioned {topic} during conversation {i} at time {i * 17}\"\n            store.add_fact(unique_text, category=FactCategory.CONTEXT)\n\n        facts = store.get_facts()\n        assert len(facts) == 100, f\"Should have exactly MAX_FACTS, got {len(facts)}\"\n\n        # The oldest facts should have been removed (they have low numbers in the text)\n        fact_0_text = f\"User mentioned {topics[0]} during conversation 0 at time 0\"\n        assert fact_0_text not in facts\n\n        # The newest fact should still be present\n        fact_104_text = f\"User mentioned {topics[104 % len(topics)]} during conversation 104 at time 1768\"\n        assert fact_104_text in facts\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_fact_metadata_persists_correctly(\n        self, memory_dir, test_user_headers\n    ):\n        \"\"\"\n        E2E: All fact metadata (category, confidence, source, timestamps) persists correctly.\n        \"\"\"\n        user_email = test_user_headers[\"x-forwarded-email\"]\n\n        # Session 1: Add a fact with all metadata\n        store1 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_email, memory_dir=memory_dir)\n        store1.load()\n\n        test_time = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n        store1.add_fact(\n            text=\"User works remotely on Tuesdays\",\n            category=FactCategory.CONTEXT,\n            confidence=0.75,\n            source=FactSource.AUTO,\n        )\n\n        # Create a store with a valid directory first\n        store = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_email, memory_dir=memory_dir)\n        store.load()\n\n        # Add a fact successfully\n        store.add_fact(\"Test fact for best effort\", category=FactCategory.PERSONAL)\n\n        # Verify it's in memory\n        facts_before = store.get_facts()\n        assert \"Test fact for best effort\" in facts_before\n\n        # Now simulate a save failure by making the directory read-only\n        # (This tests the except OSError pass in save())\n        import stat\n        memory_path = Path(memory_dir)\n        original_mode = memory_path.stat().st_mode\n\n        try:\n            # Make directory read-only\n            memory_path.chmod(stat.S_IRUSR | stat.S_IXUSR)\n\n            # Create a new store that will fail to save\n            store2 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_email, memory_dir=memory_dir)\n            store2.load()\n\n            # This add_fact will try to save but should not crash\n            # The save() will fail due to permission error, but it's caught\n            result = store2.add_fact(\"This fact will fail to save\", category=FactCategory.PERSONAL)\n\n            # The add_fact should return True (fact was added to memory)\n            # even though save() failed silently\n            assert result is True\n\n            # The fact should be in memory (added to _facts list)\n            facts_in_memory = store2.get_facts()\n            assert \"This fact will fail to save\" in facts_in_memory\n            assert \"Test fact for best effort\" in facts_in_memory\n\n        finally:\n            # Restore permissions\n            memory_path.chmod(original_mode)\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    pytest.main([__file__, \"-v\", \"--tb=short\"])\n","numLines":686,"startLine":1,"totalLines":686}}}

 \"\"\"Test gpt-5 cost calculation accuracy\"\"\"\n370\t        # Test case: 100k input, 200k output\n371\t        cost = clean_tracker.calculate_cost(\"gpt-5\", 100_000, 200_000)\n372\t\n373\t \"\"\"Test cost calculation with edge cases (0 tokens, 1 token, max tokens)\"\"\"\n403\t        # Zero tokens\n404\t        cost_zero = clean_tracker.calculate_cost(\"gpt-5\", 0, 0)\n405\t 3.0\n\n        # Second call: $2.00 more (total $4.00)\n        usage2 = await clean_tracker.track_usage(\n            session_id=session_id,\n            model=\"gpt-5\",\n            input_tokens=20_000,\n            output_tokens=60_000\n        )\n        assert usage2[\"budget_ok\"] is True\n        assert usage2[\"remaining_budget_usd\"] > 0.99\n\n        # Third call: $1.50 more (total $5.50, exceeds limit)\n        usage3 = await clean_tracker.track_usage(\n            session_id=session_id,\n            model=\"gpt-5\",\n            input_tokens=15_000,  # $0.15\n \"\"\"\n        CRITICAL TEST: Circuit breaker must activate at $100 system-wide\n        Tests coordination between multiple sessions and circuit breaker\n        \"\"\"\n        # Simulate 20 100.0\n\n        # Activate circuit breaker\n        await clean_tracker.activate_circuit_breaker()\n\n        # Verify circuit breaker state\n        assert clean_tracker.circuit_breaker_active is True\n        assert clean_tracker.circuit_breaker_reset_time is not None\n\n        # Try to check budget for new session\n        new_session = \"post-circuit-001\"\n        await clean_tracker.start_session(new_session)\n\n        budget_status = await clean_tracker.check_budget(new_session, 1.0)\n\n        # Should be blocked by circuit breaker\n        assert budget_status[\"budget_ok\"] is False\n        assert budget_status[\"circuit_breaker_active\"] is True\n        assert \"System-wide circuit breaker is active\" in budget_status[\"warnings\"]\n\n TestCostCalculationAccuracy:\n    \"\"\"Test cost calculation accuracy - must be within ±1% of OpenAI pricing\"\"\"\n\n    def test_gpt5_cost_accuracy(self, clean_tracker):\n        \"\"\"Test gpt-5 cost calculation accuracy\"\"\"\n        # Test case: 100k input, 200k output\n        cost = clean_tracker.calculate_cost(\"gpt-5\", 100_000, 200_000)\n\n        # Expected: (100k/1M * $10) + (200k/1M * $30) = $1.00 + $6.00 = $7.00\n        expected = 7.0\n        tolerance = expected * 0.01  # 1% tolerance\n\n        assert cost == pytest.approx(expected, abs=tolerance)\n\n    def test_gpt5_mini_cost_accuracy(self, clean_tracker):\n        \"\"\"Test gpt-5-mini cost calculation accuracy\"\"\"\n        # Test case: 500k input, 1M output\n        cost = clean_tracker.calculate_cost(\"gpt-5-mini\", 500_000, 1_000_000)\n\n        # Expected: (500k/1M * $3) + (1M/1M * $15) = $1.50 + $15.00 = $16.50\n        expected = 16.5\n        tolerance = expected * 0.01\n\n        assert cost == pytest.approx(expected, abs=tolerance)\n\n    def test_gpt_realtime_cost_accuracy(self, clean_tracker):\n        \"\"\"Test gpt-realtime cost calculation accuracy\"\"\"\n        # Test case: 25k input, 50k output\n        cost = clean_tracker.calculate_cost(\"gpt-realtime\", 25_000, 50_000)\n\n        # Expected: (25k/1M * $100) + (50k/1M * $200) = $2.50 + $10.00 = $12.50\n        expected = 12.5\n        tolerance = expected * 0.01\n\n        assert cost == pytest.approx(expected, abs=tolerance)\n\n    def test_cost_calculation_edge_cases(self, clean_tracker):\n        \"\"\"Test cost calculation with edge cases (0 tokens, 1 token, max tokens)\"\"\"\n        # Zero tokens\n        cost_zero = clean_tracker.calculate_cost(\"gpt-5\", 0, 0)\n        assert cost_zero == 0.0\n\n        # Single token\n        cost_one = clean_tracker.calculate_cost(\"gpt-5\", 1, 1)\n        expected_one = (1/1_000_000 * 10) + (1/1_000_000 * 30)  # $0.00004\n        assert cost_one == pytest.approx(expected_one, abs=0.0001)\n\n        # Large token counts (1M tokens)\n        cost_large = clean_tracker.calculate_cost(\"gpt-5\", 1_000_000, 1_000_000)\n mock_redis_client.delete.assert_called_with(f\"session:{session_id}\")\n\n\nclass TestPerformanceOverhead:\n    \"\"\"Test that cost tracking overhead is <5ms per request\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_cost_calculation_performance(self, clean_tracker):\n        \"\"\"Test cost calculation completes in <1ms\"\"\"\n        import time\n\n        model = \"gpt-5\"\n        input_tokens = 100_000\n        output_tokens = 200_000\n\n        start = time.perf_counter()\n        for _ in range(1000):  # 1000 iterations\n            clean_tracker.calculate_cost(model, input_tokens, output_tokens)\n        end = time.perf_counter()\n\n        avg_time_ms = ((end - start) / 1000) * 1000\n        assert avg_time_ms < 1.0  # Less than 1ms per calculation\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_track_usage_performance(self, clean_tracker):\n        \"\"\"Test track_usage completes in <5ms including metrics\"\"\"\n        import time\n\n        session_id = \"perf-test-001\"\n        await clean_tracker.start_session(session_id)\n\n        iterations = 100\n        start = time.perf_counter()\n\n        for i in range(iterations):\n            await clean_tracker.track_usage(\n                session_id=session_id,\n                model=\"gpt-5-mini\",\n                input_tokens=1000,\n                output_tokens=2000\n            )\n\n        end = time.perf_counter()\n        avg_time_ms = ((end - start) / iterations) * 1000\n\n        # Should be well under 5ms per track_usage call\n        assert avg_time_ms < 5.0\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_budget_check_performance(self, clean_tracker):\n        \"\"\"Test budget check completes in <1ms\"\"\"\n        import time\n\n        session_id = \"perf-check-001\"\n        await clean_tracker.start_session(session_id)\n\n        iterations = 1000\n        start = time.perf_counter()\n\n        for _ in range(iterations):\n            await clean_tracker.check_budget(session_id, 2.5)\n\n        end = time.perf_counter()\n        avg_time_ms = ((end - start) / iterations) * 1000\n\n        assert avg_time_ms < 1.0\n\n\nclass TestWebSocketIntegration:\n    \"\"\"Test WebSocket handler integration with cost protection\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_websocket_handler_tracks_costs(self, clean_tracker, mock_websocket):\n        \"\"\"\n        Test that WebSocket handler properly integrates cost tracking\n        Simulates real WebSocket lifecycle\n        \"\"\"\n        session_id = \"ws-integration-001\"\n\n        # Session start\n        await clean_tracker.start_session(session_id)\n        assert session_id in clean_tracker.session_costs\n\n        # Simulate API call\n        usage = await clean_tracker.track_usage(\n            session_id=session_id,\n            model=\"gpt-realtime\",\n            input_tokens=5_000,\n            output_tokens=10_000\n        )\n\n        # Send response via WebSocket\n        await mock_websocket.send_json({\n            \"type\": \"usage_update\",\n            \"session_cost\": usage[\"session_cost\"],\n            \"remaining_budget\": usage[\"remaining_budget_usd\"]\n        })\n\n        # Verify interaction\n        mock_websocket.send_json.assert_called_once()\n\n        # Session end\n        await clean_tracker.end_session(session_id)\n        assert session_id not in clean_tracker.session_costs\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_websocket_sends_budget_warning(self, clean_tracker, mock_websocket):\n        \"\"\"\n        Test WebSocket sends warning at 80% budget threshold\n        \"\"\"\n        session_id = \"ws-warning-001\"\n        await clean_tracker.start_session(session_id)\n\n        # Use $4.00 (80% of limit)\n        usage = await clean_tracker.track_usage(\n            session_id=session_id,\n            model=\"gpt-5\",\n            input_tokens=40_000,\n            output_tokens=120_000\n        )\n\n        # Check if warning should be sent\n        warning_threshold = 5.0 * 0.8\n        if usage[\"session_cost\"] >= warning_threshold and usage[\"budget_ok\"]:\n            await mock_websocket.send_json({\n                \"type\": \"budget_warning\",\n                \"message\": \"80% of session budget used\",\n                \"session_cost\": usage[\"session_cost\"],\n                \"remaining_budget\": usage[\"remaining_budget_usd\"]\n            })\n\n        # Verify warning sent\n        mock_websocket.send_json.assert_called_once()\n        call_args = mock_websocket.send_json.call_args[0][0]\n        assert call_args[\"type\"] == \"budget_warning\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_websocket_prevents_api_call_on_budget_exceeded(self, clean_tracker, mock_websocket):\n        \"\"\"\n        Test that WebSocket handler prevents API calls when budget exceeded\n        Critical for cost protection\n        \"\"\"\n        session_id = \"ws-prevent-001\"\n        await clean_tracker.start_session(session_id)\n\n        # Exceed budget\n        await clean_tracker.track_usage(\n            session_id=session_id,\n            model=\"gpt-realtime\",\n            input_tokens=30_000,\n            output_tokens=15_000\n        )\n\n        # Check budget before next API call\n        budget_status = await clean_tracker.check_budget(\n            session_id,\n            clean_tracker.session_costs[session_id]\n        )\n\n        # Should NOT make API call\n        if not budget_status[\"budget_ok\"]:\n            await mock_websocket.send_json({\n                \"type\": \"error\",\n                \"error\": \"Budget exceeded, cannot process request\"\n            })\n            await mock_websocket.close(code=1008, reason=\"Budget exceeded\")\n\n            # Verify API call blocked\n            mock_websocket.send_json.assert_called_once()\n            mock_websocket.close.assert_called_once()\n\n\nclass TestConcurrentSessionHandling:\n    \"\"\"Test concurrent session handling and isolation\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_100_concurrent_sessions(self, clean_tracker):\n        \"\"\"\n        Test handling 100 concurrent sessions with independent budgets\n        Stress test for production scenarios\n        \"\"\"\n        num_sessions = 100\n        sessions = [f\"concurrent-{i:03d}\" for i in range(num_sessions)]\n\n        # Start all sessions concurrently\n        await asyncio.gather(*[\n            clean_tracker.start_session(sid) for sid in sessions\n        ])\n\n        # Verify all sessions initialized\n        assert len(clean_tracker.session_costs) == num_sessions\n\n        # Track usage for all sessions concurrently\n        tasks = []\n        for sid in sessions:\n            tasks.append(\n                clean_tracker.track_usage(\n                    session_id=sid,\n                    model=\"gpt-5-mini\",\n                    input_tokens=10_000,\n                    output_tokens=20_000\n                )\n            )\n\n        results = await asyncio.gather(*tasks)\n\n        # Verify all sessions tracked independently\n        assert len(results) == num_sessions\n        for result in results:\n            assert result[\"budget_ok\"] is True\n            assert result[\"session_cost\"] < 1.0\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_session_isolation_under_load(self, clean_tracker):\n        \"\"\"\n        Test that session budgets remain isolated under concurrent load\n        Verifies no race conditions or budget leakage\n        \"\"\"\n        session1 = \"isolation-test-1\"\n        session2 = \"isolation-test-2\"\n\n        await clean_tracker.start_session(session1)\n        await clean_tracker.start_session(session2)\n\n        # Concurrently track different amounts\n        await asyncio.gather(\n            clean_tracker.track_usage(session1, \"gpt-5\", 50_000, 150_000),  # $5.00\n            clean_tracker.track_usage(session2, \"gpt-5-mini\", 10_000, 20_000)  # ~$0.33\n        )\n\n        # Verify costs tracked separately\n        assert clean_tracker.session_costs[session1] > 4.0\n        assert clean_tracker.session_costs[session2] < 1.0\n\n\nclass TestEdgeCasesAndErrorHandling:\n    \"\"\"Test edge cases and error {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_aadc6d7ca8b240b1a3acfcd1","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t\"\"\"\n2\tE2E integration tests for memory persistence across sessions (duck-e-genesis).\n3\t\n4\tTests the complete flow of saving a memory in one session and retrieving it\n5\tin a new session, ensuring the JSON file store correctly persists data.\n6\t\"\"\"\n7\timport json\n8\timport pytest\n9\tfrom pathlib import Path\n10\t\n11\tfrom app.memory import UserMemoryStore, FactCategory, FactSource\n12\t\n13\t\n14\tclass TestMemoryPersistenceE2E:\n15\t    \"\"\"Test that memories persist across UserMemoryStore instances (simulating new sessions).\"\"\"\n16\t\n17\t    def test_fact_persists_across_store_instances(self, tmp_path):\n18\t        \"\"\"\n19\t        E2E: A fact added in one UserMemoryStore instance is retrievable in a new instance.\n20\t\n21\t        This simulates a user saving a memory in one session and having it available\n22\t        in a subsequent session after the server restarts or a new connection is made.\n23\t        \"\"\"\n24\t        user_id = \"test@example.com\"\n25\t\n26\t        # Verify the fact is in the first session\n39\t        facts_session1 = store_session1.get_facts()\n40\t        assert \"User prefers Celsius for temperature\" in facts_session1\n41\t\n42\t        # Session 2: Create a NEW store instance (simulating a new session/connection)\n43\t        store_session2 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_id, memory_dir=str(tmp_path))\n44\t        store_session2.load()\n45\t\n46\t        # Verify the fact persisted from the previous session\n47\t        facts_session2 = store_session2.get_facts()\n48\t        assert \"User prefers Celsius for temperature\" in facts_session2\n49\t\n50\t    def test_multiple_facts_persist_across_sessions(self, tmp_path):\n51\t        \"\"\"\n52\t        E2E: Multiple facts saved in one session are all retrievable in a new session.\n53\t        \"\"\"\n54\t        user_id = \"multi-fact-user@example.com\"\n55\t\n56\t        # Session 2: Load and verify all facts persisted\n68\t        store2 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_id, memory_dir=str(tmp_path))\n69\t        store2.load()\n70\t\n71\t        facts2 = store2.get_facts()\n72\t        assert len(facts2) == 3\n73\t        assert \"User lives in London\" in facts2\n74\t        assert \"User is a software engineer\" in facts2\n75\t        assert \"User prefers dark mode\" in facts2\n76\t\n77\t    def test_fact_metadata_persists_across_sessions(self, tmp_path):\n78\t        \"\"\"\n79\t        E2E: Fact metadata (category, confidence, source) persists across sessions.\n80\t        \"\"\"\n81\t        user_id = \"metadata-test@example.com\"\n82\t\n83\t        # Session 1: Add a fact with specific metadata\n84\t        store1 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_id, memory_dir=str(tmp_path))\n85\t        store1.load()\n86\t\n87\t        store1.add_fact(\n88\t            text=\"User works remotely on Tuesdays\",\n89\t            category=FactCategory.CONTEXT,\n90\t            confidence=0.75,\n91\t            source=FactSource.AUTO,\n92\t        )\n93\t\n94\t        # User A's session\n112\t        store_a = UserMemoryStore(user_id=\"user-a@example.com\", memory_dir=str(tmp_path))\n113\t        store_a.load()\n114\t        store_a.add_fact(\"User A likes pizza\", category=FactCategory.PREFERENCE)\n115\t\n116\t        # User B's session\n117\t        store_b = UserMemoryStore(user_id=\"user-b@example.com\", memory_dir=str(tmp_path))\n118\t        store_b.load()\n119\t        store_b.add_fact(\"User B likes sushi\", category=FactCategory.PREFERENCE)\n120\t\n121\t        # Verify isolation: User A should not see User B's facts\n122\t        store_a_reload = UserMemoryStore(user_id=\"user-a@example.com\", memory_dir=str(tmp_path))\n123\t        store_a_reload.load()\n124\t        facts_a = store_a_reload.get_facts()\n125\t        assert \"User A likes pizza\" in facts_a\n126\t        assert \"User B likes sushi\" not in facts_a\n127\t\n128\t        # Verify the JSON file was created and has correct structure\n147\t        import hashlib\n148\t        user_hash = hashlib.sha256(user_id.encode()).hexdigest()\n149\t        json_path = tmp_path / f\"{user_hash}.json\"\n150\t\n151\t        assert json_path.exists(), \"Memory file should be created\"\n152\t\n153\t        with open(json_path, \"r\") as f:\n154\t            data = json.load(f)\n155\t\n156\t        assert data[\"user_id\"] == user_id\n157\t        assert \"created_at\" in data\n158\t        assert \"facts\" in data\n159\t        assert len(data[\"facts\"]) == 1\n160\t        assert data[\"facts\"][0][\"text\"] == \"Test fact\"\n161\t        assert data[\"facts\"][0][\"category\"] == \"personal\"\n162\t\n163\t        # Session 2: Reload from the JSON file\n164\t        store2 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_id, memory_dir=str(tmp_path))\n165\t        store2.load()\n166\t\n167\t        facts = store2.get_facts()\n168\t        assert \"Test fact\" in facts\n169\t\n170\t    def test_updated_at_persists_across_sessions(self, tmp_path):\n171\t        \"\"\"\n172\t        E2E: The updated_at timestamp is persisted correctly across sessions.\n173\t        \"\"\"\n174\t        user_id = \"timestamp-test@example.com\"\n175\t\n176\t        # Session 1: Create and save\n177\t        store1 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_id, memory_dir=str(tmp_path))\n178\t        store1.load()\n179\t        store1.add_fact(\"Initial fact\", category=FactCategory.PERSONAL)\n180\t\n181\t        # Get the initial updated_at\n182\t        import hashlib\n183\t        user_hash = hashlib.sha256(user_id.encode()).hexdigest()\n184\t        json_path = tmp_path / f\"{user_hash}.json\"\n185\t\n186\t        with open(json_path, \"r\") as f:\n187\t            data1 = json.load(f)\n188\t        initial_updated_at = data1.get(\"updated_at\")\n189\t\n190\t        # Session 2: Add another fact\n191\t        store2 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_id, memory_dir=str(tmp_path))\n192\t        store2.load()\n193\t        store2.add_fact(\"Second fact\", category=FactCategory.PREFERENCE)\n194\t\n195\t        # Verify updated_at changed\n196\t        with open(json_path, \"r\") as f:\n197\t            data2 = json.load(f)\n198\t        second_updated_at = data2.get(\"updated_at\")\n199\t\n200\t        assert second_updated_at is not None\n201\t        assert second_updated_at >= initial_updated_at\n202\t\n203\t    def test_contradaction_replacement_persists(self, tmp_path):\n204\t        \"\"\"\n205\t        E2E: When a fact is replaced due to contradiction, the new state persists.\n206\t        \"\"\"\n207\t        user_id = \"contradiction-test@example.com\"\n208\t\n209\t        # Session 1: Create a store, add a fact, and save\n        store_session1 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_id, memory_dir=str(tmp_path))\n        store_session1.load()\n\n        added = store_session1.add_fact(\n            text=\"User prefers Celsius for temperature\",\n            category=FactCategory.PREFERENCE,\n            confidence=0.9,\n            source=FactSource.EXPLICIT,\n        )\n        assert added, \"Fact should be added successfully\"\n\n        # Verify the fact is in the first session\n        facts_session1 = store_session1.get_facts()\n        assert \"User prefers Celsius for temperature\" in facts_session1\n\n        # Session 2: Create a NEW store instance (simulating a new session/connection)\n        store_session2 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_id, memory_dir=str(tmp_path))\n        store_session2.load()\n\n        # Verify the fact persisted from the previous session\n        facts_session2 = store_session2.get_facts()\n        assert \"User prefers Celsius for temperature\" in facts_session2\n\n    def test_multiple_facts_persist_across_sessions(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"\n        E2E: Multiple facts saved in one session are all retrievable in a new session.\n        \"\"\"\n        user_id = \"multi-fact-user@example.com\"\n\n        # Session 1: Add multiple facts\n        store1 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_id, memory_dir=str(tmp_path))\n        store1.load()\n\n        store1.add_fact(\"User lives in London\", category=FactCategory.PERSONAL)\n        store1.add_fact(\"User is a software engineer\", category=FactCategory.PERSONAL)\n        store1.add_fact(\"User prefers dark mode\", category=FactCategory.PREFERENCE)\n\n        facts1 = store1.get_facts()\n        assert len(facts1) == 3\n\n        # Session 2: Load and verify all facts persisted\n        store2 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_id, memory_dir=str(tmp_path))\n        store2.load()\n\n        facts2 = store2.get_facts()\n        assert len(facts2) == 3\n        assert \"User lives in London\" in facts2\n        assert \"User is a software engineer\" in facts2\n        assert \"User prefers dark mode\" in facts2\n\n    def test_fact_metadata_persists_across_sessions(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"\n        E2E: Fact metadata (category, confidence, source) persists across sessions.\n        \"\"\"\n        user_id = \"metadata-test@example.com\"\n\n        # Session 1: Add a fact with specific metadata\n        store1 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_id, memory_dir=str(tmp_path))\n        store1.load()\n\n        store1.add_fact(\n            text=\"User works remotely on Tuesdays\",\n            category=FactCategory.CONTEXT,\n            confidence=0.75,\n            source=FactSource.AUTO,\n        )\n\n        # Session 2: Load and verify metadata persisted\n        store2 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_id, memory_dir=str(tmp_path))\n        store2.load()\n\n        structured_facts = store2.get_structured_facts()\n        assert len(structured_facts) == 1\n\n        fact = structured_facts[0]\n        assert fact.text == \"User works remotely on Tuesdays\"\n        assert fact.category == FactCategory.CONTEXT\n        assert fact.confidence == 0.75\n        assert fact.source == FactSource.AUTO\n\n    def test_user_isolation_different_users_no_bleed(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"\n        E2E: Facts for one user do not bleed into another user's memory.\n        \"\"\"\n        # User A's session\n        store_a = UserMemoryStore(user_id=\"user-a@example.com\", memory_dir=str(tmp_path))\n        store_a.load()\n        store_a.add_fact(\"User A likes pizza\", category=FactCategory.PREFERENCE)\n\n        # User B's session\n        store_b = UserMemoryStore(user_id=\"user-b@example.com\", memory_dir=str(tmp_path))\n        store_b.load()\n        store_b.add_fact(\"User B likes sushi\", category=FactCategory.PREFERENCE)\n\n        # Verify isolation: User A should not see User B's facts\n        store_a_reload = UserMemoryStore(user_id=\"user-a@example.com\", memory_dir=str(tmp_path))\n        store_a_reload.load()\n        facts_a = store_a_reload.get_facts()\n        assert \"User A likes pizza\" in facts_a\n        assert \"User B likes sushi\" not in facts_a\n\n        # Verify isolation: User B should not see User A's facts\n        store_b_reload = UserMemoryStore(user_id=\"user-b@example.com\", memory_dir=str(tmp_path))\n        store_b_reload.load()\n        facts_b = store_b_reload.get_facts()\n        assert \"User B likes sushi\" in facts_b\n        assert \"User A likes pizza\" not in facts_b\n\n    def test_file_format_persistence_and_reload(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"\n        E2E: Verify the JSON file format is correct and can be reloaded.\n        \"\"\"\n        user_id = \"format-test@example.com\"\n\n        # Session 1: Create and save\n        store1 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_id, memory_dir=str(tmp_path))\n        store1.load()\n        store1.add_fact(\"Test fact\", category=FactCategory.PERSONAL)\n\n        # Verify the JSON file was created and has correct structure\n        import hashlib\n        user_hash = hashlib.sha256(user_id.encode()).hexdigest()\n        json_path = tmp_path / f\"{user_hash}.json\"\n\n        assert json_path.exists(), \"Memory file should be created\"\n\n        with open(json_path, \"r\") as f:\n            data = json.load(f)\n\n        assert data[\"user_id\"] == user_id\n        assert \"created_at\" in data\n        assert \"facts\" in data\n        assert len(data[\"facts\"]) == 1\n        assert data[\"facts\"][0][\"text\"] == \"Test fact\"\n        assert data[\"facts\"][0][\"category\"] == \"personal\"\n\n        # Session 2: Reload from the JSON file\n        store2 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_id, memory_dir=str(tmp_path))\n        store2.load()\n\n        facts = store2.get_facts()\n        assert \"Test fact\" in facts\n\n    def test_updated_at_persists_across_sessions(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"\n        E2E: The updated_at timestamp is persisted correctly across sessions.\n        \"\"\"\n        user_id = \"timestamp-test@example.com\"\n\n        # Session 1: Create and save\n        store1 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_id, memory_dir=str(tmp_path))\n        store1.load()\n        store1.add_fact(\"Initial fact\", category=FactCategory.PERSONAL)\n\n        # Get the initial updated_at\n        import hashlib\n        user_hash = hashlib.sha256(user_id.encode()).hexdigest()\n        json_path = tmp_path / f\"{user_hash}.json\"\n\n        with open(json_path, \"r\") as f:\n            data1 = json.load(f)\n        initial_updated_at = data1.get(\"updated_at\")\n\n        # Session 2: Add another fact\n        store2 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_id, memory_dir=str(tmp_path))\n        store2.load()\n        store2.add_fact(\"Second fact\", category=FactCategory.PREFERENCE)\n\n        # Verify updated_at changed\n        with open(json_path, \"r\") as f:\n            data2 = json.load(f)\n        second_updated_at = data2.get(\"updated_at\")\n\n        assert second_updated_at is not None\n        assert second_updated_at >= initial_updated_at\n\n    def test_contradaction_replacement_persists(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"\n        E2E: When a fact is replaced due to contradiction, the new state persists.\n        \"\"\"\n        user_id = \"contradiction-test@example.com\"\n\n        # Session 1: Add initial fact\n        store1 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_id, memory_dir=str(tmp_path))\n        store1.load()\n        store1.add_fact(\"User lives in NYC\", category=FactCategory.PERSONAL)\n\n        facts1 = store1.get_facts()\n        assert \"User lives in NYC\" in facts1\n\n        # Session 3: Verify the replacement persisted\n        store3 = UserMemoryStore(user_id=user_id, memory_dir=str(tmp_path))\n        store3.load()\n        facts3 = store3.get_facts()\n\n        assert \"User lives in Boston\" in facts3\n        assert \"User lives in NYC\" not in facts3\n","numLines":233,"startLine":1,"totalLines":233}}}
